reviews.review_text: This game is very addictive and it is huge! It took me 275 hours to play and now I have started the DLC. One of the best games I have ever played, and I have been playing games for 50 years!

[h3]A generational game, but not without flaws[/h3]

To preface this review: my only experience with any souls-like was 'The First Berserker: Khazan', and I never finished it.
Actually, I almost never finished Elden Ring either. A few hours in, and I was ready to quit, but the sheer number of players glazing this game was bugging me. "What do they see, that i dont?''
I couldn't tell you exactly when I found my answer; it was more of a gradual experience. But I do now understand, even if I don't fully agree.

The [b][i]world[/i][/b] that's built here is amazing. The ambience and overall vibes made me feel like this was a living, breathing realm full of despair and danger. Even without many cut scenes, I could still grasp the severity and gravity of the events that had taken place. (even if most of the time i had no idea what was happening in the story)

The [b][i]combat[/i][/b], while at times was a little frustrating, is still one of its best features. The build diversity and scope are something special that I can't really find a comparison to in anything else I've played before it. 
The bosses, too, were imaginative and challenging, and more than a few times I found myself jumping out of my seat and slamming my desk in excitement after I defeated them.

There's more I could praise, but I won't, because so many reviews have already done that. So instead ill bring up a few of the [b][i]negatives[/i][/b] that i dont see mentioned very often.
The target locking in this game is dreadful. I swear I died more times to this than actual bosses. It's mostly fine when you have a single enemy to deal with, but as soon as there are multiple targets, it fails miserably.
The other massive flaw I feel this game has is with its in-game systems/mechanics. I can't think of a single game I've ever played that required me to tab out and Google as many times as this. It really was ridiculous. There is just so much that isn't explained or explained well, and most players will miss out on a lot of content or waste a lot of time without Alt-tabbing and turning to guides. 

Overall though, I'm happy I put the time in and finished it. It was a great adventure.

⠀

Bought it late. Wish I hadn't waited. Played more hours than I can justify. Completed everything and immediately wanted to start again. The exploration alone is worth the price. Best gaming experience I've ever had. Just buy it. Explore slowly. Trust your curiosity. 10/10.

Elden Ring took everything that made Dark Souls great, dialled it up a bunch of notches, and set you loose in an open world.

I wasn't initially sold on the idea of an Open world souls game, but I can honestly say that I think it was a fantastic move to make the genre accessible to new players. Far more so than in the souls series, if you hit a wall you can often pick another direction very easily, find new dungeons or encounters, and hunt for loot and runes without specifically banging your head against a wall. Pair this with the spirit ashes allowing summons in almost every boss fight that you can level with you, and this is a great starting point for newcomers to the genre.

If you're a veteran, the game has you covered too, since there's no NEED to engage with summons if you don't want to. The fights escalate a lot as the game goes on, and really make you feel rewarded for learning the dances in a way I feel is only beaten by Sekiro in the Genre.
I loved it!  Such a beautiful and challenging game.  I'm going to start NG+.

In my humblest of opinions, Elden Ring is kinda... My favorite is dark souls II so make whatever assumptions you'd like, but after I first beat the game I was left feeling really meh. I couldn't bring myself to replay it the same way I want to replay DS2 or Sekiro, but I have regardless to make new characters or play with friends, just never beaten it again. Fromsoft really pushed themselves with this game, and it shows. They struggled with enemy variety in that I want to say 40% of enemies are reskinned version of other enemies, and infamously, each miniboss appears five or so times at least across the entire game. Exploration doesn't really reward the player with much, with most rewards being basic loot you can find on enemies or flowers or berry bushes and stuff. The world, however, feels a little empty. Even though dying worlds are their formula, it's really out of place here in my opinion. The game's lore revolves around the fact that the lands between are a lived in world full of colorful diverse characters, and yet I found most NPCs woefully out of place. The capitol was a huge let down for me, for example, and the roundtable hold is plain strange in my opinion, with merchants who you'll want to move between (one selling smithing stones and the other providing upgrades) being on polar opposite sides of the hub... Oh my lord and the dungeons - the dungeons as a whole are a perfect example for how tired I think the devs were while working on this game because the dungeons are the lamest and least inspired parts of this game. They are essentially the same structure with the same enemies for the most part, with ♥♥♥♥ loot at the end oh my lord. Each of them follows the same formula as well, really sad in my opinion. 

Fromsoft tried to accomplish A LOT of things with this title and I think they landed securely in an in-between place where there are great and egregious things at once, but mostly, the game is good.

I want to throw some serious shade at Shadow of the Erdtree DLC. What the hell man. It was just a slog, the areas were uninteresting, others felt uninspired and kind of pointless. The boss movesets seemed AI generated if I'm being honest, felt so unpredictable. Maybe I just wasn't good enough but holy hell the amount of shockwaves and double hit attacks (where you dodge the first part but get hit by the second while you're coming out of a roll) were commonplace. The hippo boss was insane, and I mean that in a good and bad way. It's because of all these facets I never actually reached Mesmer, just got so bogged down by those aforementioned aspects compounding onto me throughout my playtime. I've beaten Nioh 1 and 2 and those have some INSANE and irritating bosses but Shadow of the Erdtree gave me little to feel excited about in terms of pushing through. Oh my lord and the DAMAGE they deal is bonkers. Unironically, my favorite boss in all of Elden Ring was Godrick the Grafted - so much personality, BEST soundtrack in the game IMO, cool concept and phases, but whose moveset and behavior didn't feel chaotic, or plain silly like some of the bosses felt. Call me a normie, but that's my hill I guess.

Anyways after all that ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥, it's still a fantastic game, just a flawed one, as they all are. I hope that Fromsoft either improves on their tried and true formula with ideas to keep it fresh (meaning dead worlds etc) or either tries really hard to remedy what made Elden Ring suffer in my eyes - feeling empty and feeling un-lived in. I also hope they take a look back at their masterpieces like Sekiro to reach a better synthesis of their different combat systems. Elden Ring at times could've really benefitted from being more like Sekiro, but instead just felt like DS3, even with guard counters.

this is extremely replayable it has a very well done open world so it is somewhat challenging and also very entertaining to do since there are multiple O.P whepons also the spirit summons are extremely nice to distract bosses if you don't want them to fight you for a bit so genuinely think it is a great replayable game when going in for the first time so in my personal experiance 9.9/10

♥♥♥♥ u fromsoft, malenia is the worst boss to ever be created how about you learn from dark souls 3 on how to make a good boss like slave knight gael, a boss with actual readable AND dodgeable attacks. but NOOOOO lets make malenia who you literally cannot attack once before you get one shotted by waterfowl dance which is completely and utterly unavoidable. mind you i have beaten malenia twice so dont assume im some crying little kid complaining because he cant beat the boss. NO, i have suffered TWICE through that ♥♥♥♥♥ and not once have i enjoyed it
Good game, rage inducing bosses but the feeling after beating them is either bliss or annoyance.

This game was very fun. The story was engaging to me and I really enjoyed the combat. The combat can certainly be brutal at times, but it's manageable. Overall, it's a great game and I'd recommend it.

Legendary game. A masterpiece of a singleplayer game, with a bit of co-op and pvp thrown in there too.

The only real critique I personally have, is that the game is not playable at above 60 fps. I feel like today that is an actual issue, as most people with good PCs are playing games at 120+ fps and it is a big difference in smoothness and visual quality imo. 

Nevertheless, I used Lossless Scaling app to activate framegen and can play it at 120 fps that way, it works perfectly. After you have played the game once, I also highly recommend a remake mod called Elden Ring Reforged. It makes the experience better and is very easy to install. 

This game is a 10/10.
Game brings me back to childhood when my parents used to beat me black and blue. this game is torture on the soul and i love it.... thanks fromsoft
spent like half my lifespan fighting tht stupid fat dude n malenia n i hated the final boss. lwk a rlly good game tho highly recomed

Used a gigantic finger to kill a god, helped a girl breed with a dead guy and plunged the world in madness for fun.
10/10 would do it again

I have over 1500 hours across all platforms, mostly on XBOX (I transitioned to PC quite recently, thus the 52 hour playtime at the time I post this review). ELDEN RING is seriously the best game ever made.

Please dear god spend the $50 on this game. Best decision I've ever made.

All hail lord Miyazaki.
A visual and gameplay masterpiece. After a 110+ hour main story, I want to play the game over again just to try different builds.
Game is amazing. Only thing it can be improved in is an actual multiplayer mode. But currently solved with mods.

Before Elden Ring, I had never played a soulslike game. Now, after 59 hours in my first real playthrough, I finished the game. First thing I’ll say: the hype was deserved. The game is well made and my journey through [i]The Lands Between[/i]  a TON of fun. The difficulty did not hold me back at all, thanks to the generous help the game gives to the player. Are you a gamer and do you think Elden Ring will be too difficult for you? Don’t worry, just get the game. It’ll be fine.

Bullet points first, then my full spoiler-free review.

- Graphics: 8.5/10
- Exploration: 10/10
- Combat: 9/10
- Bosses: 9/10
- Audio: 9/10
- Story: 8/10
- Fun: ~9/10
- Difficulty: tough but doable

I know everyone always hypes up Elden Ring’s bosses and combat, and while they were incredibly good indeed, my personal highlight of the game was its exploration. There are so many unique areas in the game, and exploring them never feels dull: there’s LOOT everywhere, there’re QUESTS everywhere, there are DUNGEONS everywhere. The whole world is just so meticulously filled with details and things to do, that I just never got bored. My personal favorite area HAS to be [spoiler] Liurnia (duh)… Unfathomably fun. I was obsessed. [/spoiler]

My favorite part of the game is honestly just that the gameplay loop is so fun. It’s just a constant change between exploring, fighting and questing; it never gets boring. The messages people leave behind are also very fun: many of them are very funny or incredibly useful. I’d have missed so many secrets if it weren’t for messages leading me to them… It’s a wonderful replacement of using google to find your way around in the game.

The story and worldbuilding just add onto the pure EPICNESS of Elden Ring. I truly felt like a dark fantasy hero in a broken world. And the way everything matches up, everything works together towards the narrative; it’s just fantastic. 

The world itself is beautiful, we know that already, but also just incredibly BOMBASTIC and OVERWHELMING. It’s honestly a good example of the feeling of the Sublime; certain areas and places are just beyond magnificent, are just awe-inspiring on so many levels, and just so ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ gigantic. Oftentimes I wanted to go take a screenshot of some landscape but everything is just so ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ big so the screenshots never truly do the game justice. 

And now: the difficulty. The bosses are definitely hard, but thanks to the many tools the game gives you, I could beat almost every boss with less than 15 tries, often averaging 3-5 tries. And that’s not because I’m an experienced soulslike or competitive player; it’s thanks to the buffs and the loadouts you can get & thanks to the summons of the game. Otherwise I would’ve probably quit the game way before finishing it. Long live [spoiler] Moonveil & Skeleton Soldiers :)! [/spoiler]

Last few notes;
-	59 hours
-	32 achievements
-	Level 150
-	16 march – 16 may
-	Half on Steam Deck/half on PC
-	[spoiler]Ranni, Millicent, Kalé, Hewg, Roderika, Rogier, Blaidd [/spoiler] = MY GOATS
-	Fav bosses: [spoiler]Rykard, Elden Beast, Mohg, Godrick, Agheel, Godfrey[/spoiler]
-	Hated bosses: [spoiler]Deathbird, Baleful Shadow, Melania[/spoiler]
-	Hardest shardbearers: [spoiler]Melania (46), Mohg (20), Elden Beast (15)[/spoiler]
-	Easiest shardbearers: [spoiler]Renalla (1), Margit (2), Godrick (3)[/spoiler]

Elden Ring deserved game of the year. It has terrible gameplay features and the only good part it loathsome dung eater. He makes up for all the horrible moments in this game and I spend 300 of my hours in this game gooning to his beautiful body.
Really enjoyed playing the game. Ended up focusing mainly on DLC, then went back to the main game. Completed first ending for age of the stars, then went back to watch it all burn.

This review is more about a very positive impression than a game description. Not necessarily meaningful if you don't know Dark Souls games.

I love the Dark Souls games but I always got stuck at some point with about 3 possible enemies that I couldn't defeat even with reading tips. These games are hard and I guess I'm not good at this.

However, when I saw Elden Ring at a sale I was on the fence but boredom won and I didn't regret it at all. I find it so much better with the much more open world approach. If I get stuck somewhere I go back to easier places. There are so many secrets and hidden things that it's impossible to find all of them at the first or even second go through. Or I sneak around in much harder areas hoping to find better equipment without fighting (and dying).

Another aspect I really like are that you can summon ghosts that fight for you. Even if you are not good at fighting with hectic key presses, studying enemy moves and creating an effective build (all of that applies to me), over time you can upgrade your ghosts and at some point they can defeat bosses for you.

You might wonder why I like playing this if I'm so bad at this. It's the ambience, visuals and strange beings you meet. There is a really beautiful and strange world to explore. I absolutely don't mind revisiting areas  in this game if I'm (or my ghost) to weak to continue. I just enjoying playing it.

Elden Ring was my first souls game (on xbox) and it couldnt have been a better introduction into the genre.
My biggest advice to new players is: Dont look up guides on your first or even second playthrough.  Test out spells and weapons that you find and create your own builds. instead of copying a meta build from youtube or tiktok. 

The world is beautiful and full of secrets, the bosses are somewhat challenging and the game is (imo) a a game everyone needs to have experienced once in their life.
10/10.

Spent 2 hours creating my character just to get beaten to death by a golden knight on a horse in the first 2 minutes. Great game. Very relaxing before bed if you want to stare at the ceiling and question your life choices.
Great game. Nice ambience and difficulty

Good game! Great visuals, great combat, insane level of flexibility to the point that 60% of the game can be considered optional if you're good enough. Loot progression is immensely satisfying, the game make sures to make every mechanic, consumable etc useful enough that nothing feels useless. Huge replay value too, I finished the game on a strength/faith build but am already excited to try again focusing on a mage build, the freedom is crazy.

Legacy dungeons were easily my favourite part about the game, lots of hidden shortcuts and treasures with fun progression through various wild bosses, the game really encourages you to explore the world without it feeling like a constant grind. Most of all though the sheer scale of imagination was astounding, beautiful underground cities and massive mega castles  on mountaintops etc, starry night skies, cool autumn vibes, theres even a completely optional part of the map thats in a constant blizzard which is really surreal.

First time fromsoft player and its easily a solid 9/10, challenging boss fights can be made much easier by exploring the world and finding better loot etc and then coming back, to the point of sometimes nerfing yourself just to feel more challenged. My only pet peeve was the inconsistent narrative structure, the worldbuilding was incredible I just wish they utilised cutscnes more and that side quests didn't feel so cryptic sometimes. You'll have to pull up online wikis to backtrack steps and progress side quests which gets annoying but you'll get used to it, good thing is theres load of good guides online anytime you feel lost. Anyways hope you enjoy!

I could go on and on about how amazing this game is, but you can read other reviews for that. I've got a more important topic in mind.

There was so much potential, so much. The design was there. The open rpg. Everything.

And still, no sex. Tsk. Tsk. Tsk. I'm just saying. If bro and me can do somthn in co-op, it'd be a more enjoyable game.

You know, I spent so much time playing, I entirely forgot to write a review.

This game's brilliant. More than brilliant, honestly. I've played the Souls games, I'm familiar with the formula, and I feel like this game does it the best. Every build is enjoyable and rewarding in its own way - even playing as a base Wretch isn't all that awful, given the awesome r1 combos you can do with the club.

The art direction is more evolved than any Souls game I've ever played, to be honest. It knows what it wants to look like at all times and it literally never breaks that view. City design, boss design, and weapon design leave *nothing* to be desired. 

The story's great, too. I mean, it's a Souls story, so you can't expect the game to read like a Dr. Seuss novel, but once you can wrap your head around the world and its characters it's genuinely an incredibly enjoyable setting.

Honestly, this game's just great. One of the best. Exploration, gameplay, art, music, all that. You'll like it.

10/10

The mods Seamless Co-op and Reforged make it way better
Runs well enough on my hardware after a little tweaking

Great game, was hard to get into at first but teaches you not to hit everything that stands in your way. Lore actually goes crazy and the further I went into the story the more captivated I was. (Elden Ring's lore and backstory were written by George R.R. Martin, author of Game of Thrones alongside Hidetaka Miyazaki (The goat) president of FromSoftware.
By no means a noob-friendly game, the world design is S-tier once you get past the initial phase. 100% deserved the GOFTY

Not bad, but leans too much on unrealistic amounts of drags when it comes to enemies and bosses. Garbage patterns like waterfowl dance too, along with over-punishing Flask healing. Multiplayer is also broken, and PVP doesn't even compare to Dark Souls 2. Would give a mid review if possible.

Probably my most favorite game of all time.    This game provides peak thrill and perfect scenery.  I would play the most ''aura'' music and fight Godfrey and lose about 500 times but I love it still

Extremely fun and hard.
Good graphics.
Very good feeling of mastery when you beat a boss.
Smooth.
Great game, only down sides is when you quit playing for a while and have to restart a fresh build to remember how to play.

The first time I played this game I got to the first real batch of enemies and died so many times I uninstalled and refunded the game. About a year later I thought I'd give it another shot. Now I've put over 300 hours into the game, and I'd say it's one of my favorite games of all time. 

This was my first Souls game, and it took some getting used to. With a beginner's guide and the right mindset, you too can learn to embrace the pain and frustration and learn to love it.

Elden Ring, due to the open world, basically allows you to choose your difficulty. You can over-level for every single boss if you want. Or you can run to the bosses with no gear and try to kill them without getting hit. It's up to you.
 
This game, more than almost any other I've played, rewards you for everything you learn. Every time you die, you get a little better, and that is incredibly satisfying. 

The only thing I dislike and could say is a flaw is the platforming. There are times when the geometry will move you or you might not jump as far as you think. Just accept that you'll have stupid deaths from falling off ledges. 

I highly recommend this game to others who aren't sure about Souls games and would like to try them out.

This was my first souls like, and I highely recommand. The difficulty is high, but not high to the point of "I just cant". And it is somehwats bs at times but still rare enough to where its not a big issue. Other thing I love is how customizable you can make your build. I went for strength/faith hybrid and loved it. 10/10

Elden Ring is a beautifully crafted game with a captivated open world and lore that will stay with you till the end of time. I recently just finished playing through Shadow of the Erdtree and I must admit, I wish there was more. This game is so big there is plenty I have missed and I can't wait to play through it again and again.
Every time I hop on this game i either have a great time or am contemplating suicide. Best game ive ever played.
another masterpiece  form mr miyazaki with great feet and gameplay

This game is not easy but if are willing to put in the time it can get fun over time.
But be aware that especially the beginning of the game is not something I would recommend you play
without a wiki. It is an exploration game for sure and will not stop you while wasting your time.

Elden Ring feels very distinct from the rest of the Souls games and their imitators and is incredibly successful at really selling a feeling of pure adventure and exploration, on top of having a top-notch art style and combat mechanics. It's not perfect, some bosses are grueling, the map is big enough that it can be a turn-off to some, and the side quests are maddeningly easy to screw up. But it's as close to my ideal version of this game as it can get without reading my mind and that's gotta count for something.
Played it with friends, finally got to Malania got ♥♥♥♥♥♥ for 5 hours. 10/10
Truly the culmination of the souls series. Great game and a good place to start for newcomers. It might seem intimidating because of the size but just have fun and go at it.
I like the progression difficulty and the overall feel of the game i think that other would like it too
brilliant game, just beat it and got the age of stars ending im going to start the dlc soon

Absolute CINEMA!

I can't recommend this game enough, its the perfect starting place to get into the souls game, due to its expansive world and extensive resources to make yourself stronger to overcome previously difficult bosses/areas. 
10/10
great game but i've never played souls type game beforehand, so it was like having a thorny stick rammed up my ass aggressively.

This game is actually insane. If your looking at reviews to see if you should get it. GET IT. Some reviews say it has to take 110+ hours to beat. Dont listen to them. If you do every quest, every area, and everything there is to do, than yes it will take a while. But an average 1st playthrough will take upwards of 50 hours. Yes, you will 4sure use google to get going. But thats OK. Get the game and forget about the world for a bit. Have a little fun. You will only play this game for the first time once, so cherish it. This game changed my thought on single player games, let it change yours. : )
Very easy game would for sure recommend to people who don't play games or are really bad.

I played this game on my ps4 when it came out and loved it, then forgot about if until after the DLC came out. Since then, I got a PC and was a little worried that it might not work as well, but I was surprised that it ran so well. after all that I loved this game and I would recommend it to almost anyone

this game is one of the best ive ever played. aside from red dead redemption two. i would sugges it to anyone and everyone

I consider myself to be pretty cheap when buying games. I can't remember the last time I bought something that wasn't on sale. And so when I say that this game is fully worth $60, I want you to realise just how much [b] GAME [/b] is actually in this darn thing
one of the best games ever packed with content and rly fun its not as hard as the other souls games but still challenging and very beautiful

Played this for the first time on a friends PC during summer vacation. Spent 200+ hours, beat the game, and never even realized you could go to that tree area on top of the map. 
When my friend told me that the Game's hardest Boss is there, I had to buy it and start from the beginning again.

Now, I am proud to say that I have DIED 10 TIMES AND COUNTING. I. WILL. BEAT. HER!

Pretty good so far, take all the souls games and remove all the bad parts and thats this game. Big up

The screen fades to black: YOU DIED.

But this is not the end; it is the ultimate teacher. In the grand game of life, failure is inevitable, but it carries a fierce mandate: learn, adapt, and evolve.

Your mistakes are not defeats—they are blueprints for victory.

If you rise from the ashes of your errors and decode their lessons, no wall will be high enough, and no boss fearsome enough to stop you. Study the fall, forge your resolve, and conquer every single obstacle in your path!

I am absolutely obsessed with this game and love the amazing and expansive world you are immersed in from the start, even through my third playthrough I am still managing to find new areas and bosses to fight.

Speaking of bosses, just like in any other souls like game those are the best part and Elden Ring is no exception, all of them are challenging but also super fun to fight (except for the Fire Giant :P) Just PLEASE FromeSoftware add the ability to rematch bosses without having to start a NG+

Over all a super fun game and if you like a fun challenge and willing to spend some money (Worth it BTW) this is a game I would 100% recommend.
honestly  one of the greatest games of all time. if youtube didn't exist than this game would be unexplorable and unbeatable . amazing every time
Very challenging but amazing and fun game I cant stop playing. It may start a little boring but is awesome when you get into it.

Other then co-op being a huge time sink with summon signs and tarnished fingers and connection issues and having to summon the same friend after every single death (which can all be mitigated through the co-op mod) this game is a masterpiece. If you are a fan of Ocarina of Time there are gems in here that make you feel like the chef from ratatouille. If someone ever came a long and made a seamless co op that was exactly like this it would absolutely rule the genre. For now, this game coupled with said mod might be the best game of the last 12 years.

Best game in the world, When I was upset I get on the game and honestly it sounds dumb but It gave me motivation to keep going bc the tarnished keeping going forward no matter how many times he gets killed

An excellent RPG game. Very open-world and encourages experimentation among classes. Bosses and Lore are quite engaging.

Game rated 10/10!
One of the greatest video games I've ever played in my entire life and it doesn't really come close

Ive had this game for 3 years. Ive got about 8hrs in game. Ive died about 100 times. Im not good at souls-like games, never have been. 
It is a beautiful game, from what I've seen. Game mechanics are on point. All and all  its a good game.

i cant believe i havent written a review for this yet. elden ring is one of the universally best games ever made and it succeeds in perfection to an extent and scale that is genuinely incredible. i dont think there is a single other game out there that's worth your money like elden ring is
Great Souls game, 10/10 for being basically open-world.
one of if not the best game ive ever played. amazing souls experience that got me fully invested into the genre. a simply must play for anyone on the fence about buying or trying the game.

This was my first Fromsoft game. 
I was scared to play it because of the notorious difficulty their games often have, my boyfriend encouraging me to try it and friends followed. Reluctantly I gave in and now this is by far one of the best games I've ever played. Story, vibes, music, gameplay and most of all general feel. 
I find it to be a fantastic video game just across the board and recommend it to truly everyone. It's only hard if you refuse to learn from your mistakes and can't look within for your error-- It's generally very fair and forgiving when you take your time and take heart to every small visual lesson provided to you.

There's a playstyle for a little of everything. Even if you're an aggressive player like me, there's builds you can depend on and thrive. The magic feels good, the Oonga Boonga strength builds feel insane and best of all the huge assortment of incredibly designed outfits you'll find across the complexly built world. Every twist and turn rewards the greediness of adventuring-- Or punishes you delightfully for trying.

I love Elden Ring and I don't think I've left the story the same person as when I started.

Amazing game, over 322 hours played. i may come back to it soon just to finish off the last 4 achievements left. My son is old enough so I'm going to get him started on it today. cant wait to see how he does.
Great game, worth every penny, a bit difficult but very rewarding.

Got called maidenless by an NPC in the first 5 minutes of playing.
Finished the game.
Still maidenless.
Burned the world afterwards.
10/10 can recommend

Elden Ring is a great game with great gameplay, impressive graphics, and a fantastic world to explore. However, the game gives you very little guidance and doesn't tell you much about the story, which made it hard for me to stay invested.

One thing I didn't like was that you can spend hours fighting a difficult boss, only to get a 10-second cutscene as a reward. I found that pretty disappointing. The same thing happened at the end of the game as well.

In the end, Elden Ring just wasn't for me. Despite that, I still finished the entire game. My personal rating is 6/10.

Usually don't get angry with games, this made me break my controller in a fit of rage.

The DLC completely tanks my frames.

10/10 would play again.

Epic. FromSoftware has once again created an unparalleled masterpiece. It can let everyone rediscover the joy of playing Dark Souls 1 or Demon's Souls for the first time. It has a strong sense of exploration.

Elden Ring is more than just a game to me—it is a masterpiece. From the moment I stepped into the Lands Between, I felt a sense of wonder and adventure unlike anything I had experienced before. Every region, dungeon, hidden path, and mysterious corner felt worth exploring, and I made it my goal to discover every little area the world had to offer.

What makes Elden Ring so special is the freedom it gives players. The world is vast, beautiful, and filled with secrets that reward curiosity. Every victory against a difficult boss felt earned, and every new discovery created a memorable experience. The atmosphere, music, lore, and world design all come together perfectly to create an unforgettable journey.

The Lands Between became a place where I created many great memories. Whether it was finding a hidden cave, overcoming a challenging enemy after countless attempts, or simply riding across the breathtaking landscapes, the game constantly gave me moments that stayed with me long after I put down the controller.

For me, Elden Ring is not just one of the best games I have played—it is an adventure I will always remember. It is a rare game that inspires exploration, rewards perseverance, and leaves players with stories of their own. A true masterpiece.

I finished the base game, got 100% achievements, completed Shadow of the Erdtree, and somehow I still want to keep playing.

Elden Ring took me a while to really understand. I was confused at first, especially with the builds, quests, and where to go, but once it clicked, it became really hard to put down.

The exploration is probably my favorite part. There’s always something weird, cool, or unexpected waiting somewhere, and the game keeps rewarding you for being curious. The combat also feels great, especially once you find a build that fits you.

It’s not perfect. Some quests are too vague, some bosses can be really annoying, and the DLC can feel brutal at first. But even with those things, this is easily one of the best games I’ve ever played.

130 hours in, and I still don’t feel completely done with it.
